name: Abalone Meuniere Mandarin
recipeInstructions:
The following four recipes are from a Chron article called "Foreign Intrigue" by Alice Cuneo that featured recipes from various consulates in the City. This first one is from the Consulate of the People's Republic of China.
1. Prepare the abalone: Trim the abalone, removing any rough parts and cut into flat rounds.
2. Prepare the filling: Chop shrimp and fat pork to a paste consistency; stir in the water chestnuts, parsley, and ginger water. Season with salt and MSG.
3. Assemble the "sandwiches": Spread an abalone round with shrimp paste, then top with another round of abalone.
4. Coat the "sandwiches" with corn flour by tossing them in a shallow dish filled with corn flour.
5. Heat chive and ginger oil in a wok over medium heat until hot but not smoking. Swirl the oil to coat all sides of the wok.
6. Dip each abalone "sandwich" first in the corn flour, then in beaten eggs.
7. Carefully place the coated sandwiches into the hot oil one at a time and fry until golden brown and crispy. This should take about 2-3 minutes per side. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
8. In the same wok, add Shaoxing wine, chicken stock, salt, and MSG. Bring to a simmer.
9. Add the fried abalone "sandwiches" back into the wok and continue to simmer for 3-4 minutes until all ingredients are heated through.
10. Adjust seasonings as needed, then serve immediately.
NOTE: Chive and ginger oil is oil in which chives and ginger have been cooked. To make at home, heat 4 ounces of oil in a wok over medium heat. Toss in 1-2 tablespoons of fresh snipped chives and 3 slices of fresh ginger. Stir-fry until fragrant, then proceed with the recipe. Remove ginger slices before serving. Ginger water can be found in Chinese markets.
